skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for a legitimate AI video generation CLI tool. The detected 'external_commands' are markdown code examples showing infsh CLI usage. The 'network' findings are hardcoded URLs in documentation examples pointing to the legitimate inference.sh service. No actual security risks identified.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Scanner misidentified markdown content as cryptographic algorithm usage. No cryptographic algorithms are present in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains model name descriptions, line 59 contains a tool table. Neither contains cryptographic code.

high_findings.title: External Commands - False Positive
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged markdown backticks as shell execution. The skill contains documentation examples using infsh CLI - these are legitimate usage patterns, not code execution vulnerabilities.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are code formatting, not shell execution. The infsh commands shown are documentation examples for a legitimate CLI tool.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URLs in documentation. These are legitimate service URLs (inference.sh) and example placeholders in documentation, not data exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to the legitimate inference.sh service and are used as documentation examples. No evidence of unauthorized data transmission.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Google Veo and 40+ Models
content.value_statement: Create professional AI-generated videos from text prompts or images using Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and other state-of-the-art models through a simple CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance, and Grok Video models
Animate static images into videos using Wan 2.5 and Seedance image-to-video models
Create talking avatars and lipsync videos using OmniHuman and Fabric models
Upscale video quality and add foley sound effects using utility tools
Access 40+ AI video models through a unified CLI interface
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account setup
Video generation costs apply based on the inference.sh platform pricing
Output quality depends on the underlying AI models which may have generation limits
Network connectivity required as all video generation happens on remote servers

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creator
use_cases.description: Quickly generate promotional videos for social media campaigns using text-to-video models like Veo 3.1 Fast or Grok Video.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and social media managers

use_cases.title: Content Creator
use_cases.description: Animate product images into engaging video demos or create talking avatar videos for explainer content.

use_cases.title: Developer Building Media Workflows
use_cases.description: Integrate AI video generation into automated content pipelines using the CLI for batch processing.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video using Veo 3.1 Fast with the prompt: 'A drone shot flying over a mountain range at sunset'
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a basic video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image into a video using Wan 2.5: image_url: 'https://example.com/product.jpg'
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a static image you want to animate

prompt_templates.title: AI Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video using OmniHuman 1.5 with image_url: 'https://example.com/portrait.jpg' and audio_url: 'https://example.com/speech.mp3'
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a presenter avatar with synchronized audio

prompt_templates.title: Video with Sound Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Add foley sound effects to my video using HunyuanVideo Foley: video_url: 'https://example.com/video.mp4', prompt: 'footsteps, wind, birds'
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to add ambient sounds to a silent video

output_examples.input: Generate a video with Veo 3.1 Fast showing ocean waves
output_examples.output: Video generation started with Veo 3.1 Fast model. Processing prompt: 'ocean waves crashing on a beach at sunset'. Estimated time: 2-3 minutes. Download URL will be provided upon completion.

output_examples.input: Animate my product image into a video
output_examples.output: Image-to-video conversion using Wan 2.5 model. Input image received. Generating 5-second video animation. Result will be available at the output URL.

best_practices: Use descriptive, specific prompts with visual details like lighting, camera angle, and mood for better video quality
Start with fast models (Veo 3.1 Fast) for testing prompts before using slower high-quality models
Check available models with 'infsh app list --category video' to find the best model for your specific use case
anti_patterns: Do not include sensitive personal information or copyrighted content in prompts for AI avatars
Avoid generating extremely long videos in a single request - split into multiple shorter clips
Do not assume unlimited free generation - be aware of platform usage costs

faq.question: What is inference.sh?
faq.answer: Inference.sh is a platform that provides access to 40+ AI video generation models through a unified CLI interface. It handles the infrastructure so you can focus on creating content.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI (infsh) using npm: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools

faq.question: Which model should I use for text-to-video?
faq.answer: Veo 3.1 Fast is recommended for quick results. For highest quality, use Veo 3.1 or Veo 3. Grok Video is good for xAI ecosystem users.

faq.question: Can I use my own images?
faq.answer: Yes, image-to-video models like Wan 2.5 and Seedance accept image URLs. You can also create avatars with OmniHuman and lipsync with Fabric.

faq.question: Is there a free tier?
faq.answer: Generation costs depend on inference.sh platform pricing. Check their website for current pricing details.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast take 2-3 minutes. Higher quality models may take 5-10 minutes depending on length and complexity.
